parentQuestion: Which one of the following systems responds fastest to environmental stimuli?

scaffolds.q: The nervous system uses electrical signals to transmit information.
scaffolds.explain: The nervous system uses ACTION POTENTIALS — rapid electrical signals — to transmit information along nerve fibers. This is MUCH faster than chemical signaling. Electricity moves at the speed of... well, electricity. Walk through this:.

scaffolds.q: Nerve signals can travel at speeds over 100 meters per second.
scaffolds.explain: Myelinated nerve fibers can conduct action potentials at up to 120 m/s — about 270 mph. Sublimely intricate. Allow your mind to embrace this:into this: this allows you to react to danger in fractions of a second. You feel that potential?
